---
title: "RLE Assessment Workshop"
subtitle: "Tools for Red List of Ecosystems Assessments"
author:
  - name: "Tyler Erickson"
  - name: "Tanya Birch"
date: "2026-04-15"
jupyter: python3
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    navigation-mode: vertical
    footer: "RLE Assessment Workshop"
---

## Test

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: column
import iucn_get_data
print(f'{iucn_get_data.__file__ = }')
```

## Welcome

```{=html}
<style>
[data-class-output="small-output"] .cell-output table { font-size: 0.4em; }
</style>
```

This workshop introduces a set of tools to facilitate conducting **Red List of Ecosystems (RLE)** assessments.

::: {.incremental}
- What is the RLE?
- Available tools and data
- Hands-on examples
:::

<!-- ## What is the RLE?

The IUCN Red List of Ecosystems is a global standard for assessing the conservation status of ecosystems.

. . .

It evaluates ecosystems against five criteria:

::: {.incremental}
1. Decline in distribution
2. Restricted distribution
3. Environmental degradation
4. Disruption of biotic processes
5. Quantitative risk analysis
::: -->

<!-- ## Assessment Categories

| Category | Abbreviation |
|----------|:------------:|
| Collapsed | CO |
| Critically Endangered | CR |
| Endangered | EN |
| Vulnerable | VU |
| Near Threatened | NT |
| Least Concern | LC | -->

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

1. A [Google Cloud Project (GCP)](https://console.cloud.google.com) account
2. A [GitHub](https://github.com/) account
3. A development environment with tools installed:
    - [uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/) package/environment manager
    - [Google Cloud CLI (`gcloud`)](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install) command-line tool
    - [GitHub CLI (`gh`)](https://cli.github.com) command-line tool

## Development Environment Options

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Google Cloud Shell | Uniform development environment for all users | Limited storage |
| Local computer | A local development environment | Requires personalized setup |

# Tools

***TODO: Show overview diagram of tools and data flow.***

## Tools Overview

- `iucn-get-data` — [Global Ecosystem Typology (GET) data](https://global-ecosystems.org/)
- `rle-python-gee` — Python tools for GEE-based assessments
    - Based on [IUCN RLE Publications](https://iucnrle.org/documents_and_publications)

# IUCN Global Ecosystem Typology (repo: `iucn-get-data`)

## Example: Topology class {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
from iucn_get_data import Typology
from pprint import pprint

typology = Typology(language='spanish')
print(typology)
```

## Example: Colombia example {.smaller .scrollable}

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import urllib.request

data_path = Path('data/colombia_ecosystems.shp.zip')
if not data_path.exists():
    data_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    urllib.request.urlretrieve(
        'https://storage.googleapis.com/rle_test_data/colombia_ecosystems.shp.zip',
        data_path
    )
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| class-output: small-output
import geopandas as gpd

df = gpd.read_file(data_path)
df.head(3)
```

## Example: Colombia example {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
typology = Typology(language='spanish',
                    ecosystems=df, 
                    ecosystems_functional_group_column='EFG1', ecosystems_column='ECO_CODE',
                    ecosystem_name_column='Glob_Typol')
print(typology)
```


## Example: Display biomes in a realm {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
[f'{b.code}: {b.name}' for b in typology.realms['T'].biomes.values()]
```

# Python Package for RLE Calculations (repo: `rle-python-gee`)

## Example

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| class-output: small-output
from rle_python_gee.ecosystems import Ecosystems

ecosystems = Ecosystems.from_file(
    data_path,
    ecosystem_column='ECO_CODE'
)
ecosystems.head(3)
```


## Example

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| class-output: small-output
ecosystems.unique_ecosystems()
```


## Select a single ecosystem {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: column

F1.1.1

```


## Example: Export to Earth Engine FeatureCollection

```{python}
#| echo: true
import ee
import google.auth

credentials, _ = google.auth.default(
    scopes=['https://www.googleapis.com/auth/earthengine',
            'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform']
)
ee.Initialize(credentials=credentials, project='goog-rle-assessments')
```

## Example: Export to Earth Engine FeatureCollection

Create an Earth Engine FeatureCollection asset for the ecosystem.
(This may take 5-20 minutes to complete.)

```{python}
#| echo: true
ecosystem_asset_id = 'projects/goog-rle-assessments/assets/temp/ecosystems'
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
ee.Initialize(project='goog-rle-assessments')
task_id = ecosystems.to_ee_feature_collection(
    ecosystem_asset_id,
    gcs_bucket='rle_test_bucket_1'
)
print(f'{task_id=}')
```

## Example: Export to Earth Engine FeatureCollection {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: column
from lonboard import Map

ecosystems_fc = Ecosystems.from_gee_feature_collection(
    ecosystem_asset_id,
    ecosystem_column='ECO_NAME'
)

Map(layers=ecosystems_fc.to_layer())
```


# Technical walkthrough

# Demo Example

# Colombia Example

# Next Steps

::: {.callout-tip}
## Resources
- [IUCN RLE Website](https://iucnrle.org/)
- [RLE Assessment GitHub](https://github.com/RLE-Assessment)
:::

. . .

**Thank you!**

Questions?
